entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary crop for which planting and fertilizer management decisions are analyzed.

entities.name: GCM-BASED SEASONAL RAINFALL FORECASTS
entities.description: General circulation model-based predictions of precipitation used to inform agricultural management.

entities.name: KATUMANI
entities.description: A semi-arid location in Southern Kenya used for the study.

entities.name: MAKINDU
entities.description: A semi-arid location in Southern Kenya used for the study.

entities.name: SOUTHERN KENYA
entities.description: The broader geographic region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: CROP YIELD SIMULATION
entities.description: A technique used to estimate the yield of maize based on various inputs and weather conditions.

entities.name: STOCHASTIC ENTERPRISE BUDGETING
entities.description: An economic analysis method used to identify profit-maximizing rates and densities.

entities.name: STOCHASTIC DOMINANCE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to evaluate the preference of risk-averse farmers for specific management strategies.

entities.name: FERTILIZER N RATES
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen fertilizer applied to the crop.

entities.name: STAND DENSITIES
entities.description: The density of plants per unit area in the field.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced, influenced by rainfall, nitrogen supply, and stand density.

entities.name: PROFIT
entities.description: The financial gain from maize production, measured by gross margins and gross value of production.

entities.name: SEA SURFACE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Boundary conditions used in GCMs to determine the predictability of rainfall forecasts.

entities.name: GRAIN PRICE
entities.description: The market price of maize, which significantly affects the value of forecasts.

entities.name: INPUT COSTS
entities.description: The costs associated with production inputs like fertilizer.

entities.name: RISK AVERSION
entities.description: The degree to which farmers avoid uncertainty in their management decisions.
